channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Greetings from Syria, where a postwar olive harvest offers a long-lost taste of home
item.description: In the warm sun, gathering handfuls of hard olives promised a taste of home that residents of a village in the Homs countryside had been missing for nearly 14 years of civil war.
item.content:encoded: In the warm sun, gathering handfuls of hard olives promised a taste of home that residents of a village in the Homs countryside had been missing for nearly 14 years of civil war.
item.dc:creator: Emily Feng

item.title: The Trump team is quietly eliminating U.S. support for birth control abroad
item.description: Congress has allocated more than $500 million for family planning work internationally. The Trump administration hasn't spent it — and the consequences are already being felt.
item.content:encoded:
Congress has allocated more than $500 million for family planning work internationally. The Trump administration hasn't spent it — and the consequences are already being felt.

(Image credit: Edward Echwalu for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Gabrielle Emanuel

item.title: Welcome to 'Anxietyland' theme park, where the rides are no fun
item.description: From the Emotional Roller Coaster to the Worry-go-round, cartoonist Gemma Correll walks us through her brain's not-so-amusing amusement park in a darkly funny memoir.
item.content:encoded: From the Emotional Roller Coaster to the Worry-go-round, cartoonist Gemma Correll walks us through her brain's not-so-amusing amusement park in a darkly funny memoir.
item.dc:creator: LA Johnson

item.title: Families sue OpenAI over Canadian mass shooter's use of ChatGPT
item.description: The lawsuits claim OpenAI was negligent for failing to report the shooter to authorities after her account was flagged for "gun violence activity and planning."
item.content:encoded:
The lawsuits claim OpenAI was negligent for failing to report the shooter to authorities after her account was flagged for "gun violence activity and planning."

(Image credit: Paige Taylor White)
item.dc:creator: Geoff Brumfiel

item.title: Baby teeth hold clues to the harms of toxic metals for infants — and older kids
item.description: By analyzing layers in these teeth, scientists have pinpointed a critical window when baby brains are most vulnerable to toxic metals — and linked that to behavior problems later in life.
item.content:encoded: By analyzing layers in these teeth, scientists have pinpointed a critical window when baby brains are most vulnerable to toxic metals — and linked that to behavior problems later in life.
item.dc:creator: Maria Godoy

item.title: SCOTUS weighs Temporary Protected Status cases. And, jury indicts James Comey again
item.description: The Supreme Court is set to hear arguments on whether to end Temporary Protected Status for Haitians and Syrians. And, a grand jury has indicted former FBI Director James Comey for a second time.
item.content:encoded:
The Supreme Court is set to hear arguments on whether to end Temporary Protected Status for Haitians and Syrians. And, a grand jury has indicted former FBI Director James Comey for a second time.

(Image credit: Dia Dipasupil)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: South Korean court sentences ex-President Yoon to 7 years in prison
item.description: An appeals court sentenced ousted President Yoon Suk Yeol to 7 years in prison for resisting arrest and bypassing a Cabinet meeting before his brief imposition of martial law in December 2024.
item.content:encoded:
An appeals court sentenced ousted President Yoon Suk Yeol to 7 years in prison for resisting arrest and bypassing a Cabinet meeting before his brief imposition of martial law in December 2024.

(Image credit: Ahn Young-joon)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Spirit Airlines tried to be the Dollar General of the skies. Then the big airlines beat it at its own game
item.description: Spirit Airlines helped pioneer ultra-cheap flying and soared. Then legacy airlines copied them, outmaneuvered them with loyalty programs, and the economy turned against their core customers.
item.content:encoded:
Spirit Airlines helped pioneer ultra-cheap flying and soared. Then legacy airlines copied them, outmaneuvered them with loyalty programs, and the economy turned against their core customers.

(Image credit: Justin Sullivan)
item.dc:creator: Greg Rosalsky

item.title: Like soap operas, 75 feet up: How bald eagle nest cams hook online communities
item.description: Across the country, some 50 bald eagle nests fitted with cameras broadcast up-close views of raptor family life. Every spring, as eggs hatch and eaglets grow, these cameras rake in millions of views.
item.content:encoded: Across the country, some 50 bald eagle nests fitted with cameras broadcast up-close views of raptor family life. Every spring, as eggs hatch and eaglets grow, these cameras rake in millions of views.
item.dc:creator: Ava Berger

item.title: RFK Jr. talked about 'reparenting' kids on wellness farms. We visit one that inspired him
item.description: U.S. Secretary of Health and Human Services Robert F. Kennedy Jr. says a farm community in Italy for people with addiction is a model for wellness camps designed to ease the U.S. overdose crisis. Critics say the idea is dangerous.
item.content:encoded:
U.S. Secretary of Health and Human Services Robert F. Kennedy Jr. says a farm community in Italy for people with addiction is a model for wellness camps designed to ease the U.S. overdose crisis. Critics say the idea is dangerous.

(Image credit: Elisabetta Zavoli)
item.dc:creator: Brian Mann
